title: Phase 0 - Protocol V1 and architecture draft
goal: Freeze the initial architectural model and define the first executable protocol contract for headless and desktop adapters.

tasks.title: Freeze 4-layer architecture draft
tasks.description: Confirm the conceptual boundaries for Core, Engine, Ports/Contracts, and UI Adapters.
notes: The agreed layers are Core, Engine, Ports/Contracts, and UI Adapters
Textual is transitional only
Desktop and headless are the primary adapter targets
acceptance_criteria: Each layer has a clear responsibility

tasks.title: Define protocol V1 scope
tasks.description: Define what belongs to the first protocol version and what is explicitly out of scope.
notes: The first version must stay intentionally small
Both event stream mode and final result mode are included
Desktop v1 will consume the same protocol through a local subprocess
acceptance_criteria: V1 includes events, commands, prompt requests, and final result
The scope is written down explicitly

tasks.title: Define outbound event model
tasks.description: List and describe the first set of engine events emitted during workflow execution.
notes: A first draft exists with run, step, output, prompt, and terminal events

tasks.title: Define inbound command model
tasks.description: List and describe the first set of commands that adapters can send to the engine.
notes: A first draft exists with start_run, submit_prompt_response, and cancel_run
It is still open whether start_run belongs inside the runtime protocol or remains an adapter-level action
acceptance_criteria: start_run is either accepted or explicitly left outside the runtime protocol

tasks.title: Define PromptRequest model
tasks.description: Define the prompt contract including supported prompt kinds for V1.
notes: A first draft exists
No final restriction has been made yet on the exact V1 prompt kinds
acceptance_criteria: Supported kinds for V1 are listed
Option structure is defined for future selection prompts

tasks.title: Define OutputPayload model
tasks.description: Define the semantic output types exposed by the engine.
notes: A first draft exists
It is still open whether V1 output content should remain string-only
acceptance_criteria: Any deferred kinds are explicitly noted

tasks.title: Define FinalResult model
tasks.description: Define the terminal run snapshot returned by headless and consumed by adapters.
notes: A first draft exists with run_id, workflow_name, status, steps, result, and diagnostics
acceptance_criteria: Final outputs and diagnostics are included

tasks.title: Define transport strategy for headless V1
tasks.description: Clarify how the protocol is transported in headless mode.
notes: Both final result mode and event stream mode are required
stdout is reserved for protocol JSON and stderr for diagnostics
acceptance_criteria: JSON Lines event stream mode is defined

tasks.title: Define desktop PoC integration model
tasks.description: Describe how the desktop client communicates with Titan in the first iteration.
notes: Desktop v1 uses a local subprocess
The desktop consumes the event stream and submits commands/responses back
acceptance_criteria: The desktop launches Titan as a subprocess
The desktop consumes the event stream
The desktop submits prompt responses back to the engine

tasks.title: Define workflow screen PoC scope
tasks.description: Freeze the minimal UX scope for the first workflow execution screen.
notes: A first draft exists with header, step list, output panel, prompt panel, and actions
acceptance_criteria: The screen shows the active prompt

tasks.title: Choose desktop technology for the PoC
tasks.description: Confirm the desktop UI technology to use for the first adapter prototype.
notes: Kotlin/Compose Desktop is the current preferred direction
Tauri was considered as the main alternative
The choice is based on stronger Kotlin/Android familiarity and expected implementation speed
acceptance_criteria: The preferred desktop technology is chosen

tasks.title: Assess Compose Desktop viability and distribution cost
tasks.description: Evaluate the current robustness of Compose Desktop and the practical cost of distributing desktop builds.
notes: Compose Desktop is considered robust enough for a Titan desktop client
Distribution is viable but requires OS-specific builds
macOS signing and notarization remain a real operational cost
Packaging uses jpackage/jlink through the JetBrains plugin
